speaker: medium/framing
segment_text: Statement attributed to the dead man as conveyed through a medium.
context_note: Framing segment records mediation; it is not a direct claim by the samurai.
copyright_note: Paraphrased framing created for packet metadata.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: While I was sitting bound there, my wife was with the robber in the grove.
context_note: Compressed from the samurai testimony; establishes bound vantage point and mediation.
copyright_note: Short adapted excerpt for review; verify translation status before publication.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: She said, "Then take me away with you wherever you go."
context_note: Direct quoted statement attributed by the samurai to his wife.
copyright_note: From 1952 Kojima translation as hosted by Wikisource; review required.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: She pointed at me tied to the cedar and cried, "Kill him! I cannot marry you as long as he lives."
copyright_note: From 1952 Kojima translation as hosted by Wikisource; review required.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: The robber looked at me and asked whether she should be killed or saved.
context_note: Samurai reports the robber asking him what to do with the wife.
copyright_note: Short adapted excerpt for review; verify translation status before publication.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: While I hesitated, she shrieked and ran into the depths of the grove.
copyright_note: From 1952 Kojima translation as hosted by Wikisource; review required.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: After she ran away, he took up my sword, and my bow and arrows.
copyright_note: From 1952 Kojima translation as hosted by Wikisource; review required.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: With a single stroke he cut one of my bonds.
context_note: Samurai reports partial release by robber.
copyright_note: From 1952 Kojima translation as hosted by Wikisource; review required.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: Then he disappeared from the grove.
copyright_note: From 1952 Kojima translation as hosted by Wikisource; review required.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: Untying the rest of my bonds, I listened carefully, and noticed that it was my own crying.
context_note: Samurai reports crying after robber leaves.
copyright_note: From 1952 Kojima translation as hosted by Wikisource; review required.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: I took up the small sword which my wife had dropped and stabbed it into my breast.
copyright_note: Short adapted excerpt for review; verify translation status before publication.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: When my breast grew cold, everything was as silent as the dead in their graves.
context_note: Samurai reports bodily coldness and silence after stabbing.
copyright_note: From 1952 Kojima translation as hosted by Wikisource; review required.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: Someone crept up to me and drew the small sword softly out of my breast.
context_note: Samurai reports an unidentified later action after the self-stabbing account.
copyright_note: Short adapted excerpt for review; verify translation status before publication.

speaker: dead_samurai_via_medium
segment_text: At the same time once more blood flowed into my mouth, and I sank down into the darkness of space.
context_note: Samurai reports final sinking into darkness after the unidentified action.
copyright_note: From 1952 Kojima translation as hosted by Wikisource; review required.
